name: Eye of Ra
shortDescription: The Eye of Ra is a powerful symbol in Egyptian mythology representing the sun god Ra's destructive and protective power. It is often personified as a goddess who enacts Ra's will and defends him from his enemies.
description: The Eye of Ra (also known as the Eye of Re) is a powerful and complex symbol in ancient Egyptian mythology, representing the sun god Ra's feminine counterpart and an extension of his power. It embodies both destructive and protective forces, acting as Ra's agent of vengeance against those who defy his divine order. The Eye is often personified as a goddess, most commonly Sekhmet, Hathor, Bastet, or Tefnut, each embodying different aspects of the Eye's multifaceted nature. Its fiery nature is associated with the scorching heat of the sun and the power to incinerate enemies. The Eye's departure from Ra and subsequent return is a recurring motif in Egyptian mythology, often linked to stories of reconciliation and the restoration of cosmic balance. The Eye's anger and destructive potential must be appeased to ensure harmony. The Uraeus, the cobra on the brow of pharaohs, is also associated with the Eye of Ra, symbolizing royal power and protection. The Eye of Ra is distinct from the Eye of Horus (Wedjat), which represents healing, protection, and restoration and is associated with the god Horus. Although distinct, both Eyes share a common theme of divine power and protection, representing different aspects of Egyptian cosmology and divine authority. The Eye of Ra emphasizes the sun god's power and his ability to punish wrongdoing and maintain cosmic order.

sources.title: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Wilkinson, Richard H.
